channel.title: Lecture Notes on MIT OpenCourseWare
channel.description: Recent content in Lecture Notes on MIT OpenCourseWare

item.title: Applications: Chemical and Phase Equilibria
item.description: Lecture notes on chemical and phase equilibria. The objective of this lecture is to apply statistical mechanics to develop microscopic models for problems you’ve treated so far with macroscopic thermodynamics.

item.title: Applications: Chemical and Phase Equilibria
item.description: Lecture notes on chemical and phase equilibria. The objective of this lecture is to apply statistical mechanics to develop microscopic models for problems you’ve treated so far with macroscopic thermodynamics.

item.title: Calorimetry
item.description: Lecture notes on calorimetry and bond energies.

item.title: Calorimetry
item.description: Lecture notes on calorimetry, constant volume, and bond energies.

item.title: Chemical Equilibrium
item.description: Lecture notes on Helmholtz and Gibbs free energy, chemical equilibrium, ideal gases, and the effect of total pressure.

item.title: Chemical Equilibrium
item.description: Lecture notes on Helmholtz and Gibbs free energy, chemical equilibrium, ideal gases, and the effect of total pressure.

item.title: Chemical Oscillations
item.description: Lecture notes on chemical oscillations and autocatalysis.

item.title: Clausius-Clapeyron Equation
item.description: Lecture notes on the Clausius-Clapeyron equation.

item.title: Clausius-Clapeyron Equation
item.description: Lecture notes on the Clausius-Clapeyron equation.

item.title: Colligative Properties
item.description: Lecture notes on colligative properties, vapor pressure lowering, boiling point elevation, freezing point depression, and osmotic pressure.

item.title: Colligative Properties
item.description: Lecture notes on colligative properties, vapor pressure lowering, boiling point elevation, freezing point depression, and osmotic pressure.

item.title: Complex Reactions and Mechanisms
item.description: Lecture notes on complex reactions and mechanisms.

item.title: Complex Reactions and Mechanisms (continued)
item.description: Lecture notes on complex reactions and mechanisms.

item.title: Complex Reactions and Mechanisms (continued)
item.description: Lecture notes on complex reactions and mechanisms, and steady-state and equilibrium approximations.

item.title: Complex Reactions and Mechanisms (continued)
item.description: Lecture notes on complex reactions and mechanisms.

item.title: Complex Reactions and Mechanisms (continued)
item.description: Lecture notes on complex reactions and mechanisms.

item.title: Complex Reactions and Mechanisms (continued)
item.description: Lecture notes on complex reactions and mechanisms, and steady-state and equilibrium approximations.

item.title: Complex Reactions and Mechanisms (continued)
item.description: Lecture notes on complex reactions and mechanisms.

item.title: Criteria for Spontaneous Change
item.description: Lecture notes on criteria for spontaneous change.

item.title: Criteria for Spontaneous Change
item.description: Lecture notes on criteria for spontaneous change.

item.title: Enthalpy
item.description: Lecture notes on enthalpy, chemical reactions and biological processes, and Joule-Thomson expansion.

item.title: Enthalpy
item.description: Lecture notes on enthalpy, chemical reactions and biological processes, and Joule-Thomson expansion.

item.title: Entropy
item.description: Lecture notes on entropy.

item.title: Entropy
item.description: Lecture notes on entropy.

item.title: Entropy and Disorder
item.description: Lecture notes on entropy, disorder, and the Clausius inequality.

item.title: Entropy and Disorder
item.description: Lecture notes on entropy, disorder, and the Clausius inequality.

item.title: Enzyme Catalysis
item.description: Lecture notes on enzyme catalysis, the Michaelis-Menten mechanism, and the Lineweaver-Burk plot.

item.title: Enzyme Catalysis
item.description: Lecture notes on enzyme catalysis, the Michaelis-Menten mechanism, and the Lineweaver-Burk plot.

item.title: Equilibrium in Solution
item.description: Lecture notes on equilibrium in solution.

item.title: Equilibrium in Solution
item.description: Lecture notes on equilibrium in solution.

item.title: Equilibrium: Application to Drug Design
item.description: Lecture notes about chemical equilibrium in gases and application to drug design.

item.title: Equilibrium: Application to Drug Design
item.description: Lecture notes about chemical equilibrium in gases and application to drug design.

item.title: Fundamental Equations, Absolute Entropy, and The Third Law
item.description: Lecture notes on fundamental equations, absolute entropy, and the third law of thermodynamics.

item.title: Fundamental Equations, Absolute Entropy, and The Third Law
item.description: Lecture notes on fundamental equations, absolute entropy, and the third law of thermodynamics.

item.title: Isothermal Gas Expansion
item.description: Lecture notes on isothermal gas expansion, internal energy, expansion work, and Joule free expansion of a gas.

item.title: Isothermal Gas Expansion
item.description: Lecture notes on isothermal gas expansion, internal energy, expansion work, and Joule free expansion of a gas.

item.title: Kinetics: Reaction Rates, Orders, and Half Lives
item.description: Lecture notes on kinetics, reaction rates, orders, and half lives.

item.title: Kinetics: Reaction Rates, Orders, Half Lives
item.description: Lecture notes on kinetics, reaction rates, orders, and half lives.

item.title: Lecture Notes
item.description:
The lecture handouts are available in the table below. Please note that there were no lecture notes for session #36.

SES # TOPICS LECTURE NOTES
1 State of a system, 0th law, equation of state (PDF)
2 Work, heat, first law (PDF)
3 Internal energy, expansion work (PDF)
4 Enthalpy (PDF)
5 Adiabatic changes (PDF)
6 Thermochemistry (PDF)
7 Calorimetry (PDF)
8 Second law (PDF)

item.title: Model Systems
item.description: Lecture notes on model systems, partition functions, statistical mechanics, and discrete energy levels.

item.title: Model Systems
item.description: Lecture notes on model systems, partition functions, statistical mechanics, and discrete energy levels.

item.title: Multicomponent Systems, Partial Molar Quantities, and the Chemical Potential
item.description: Lecture notes about criteria for equilibrium, multicomponent systems, partial molar quantities, and the chemical potential.

item.title: Multicomponent Systems, Partial Molar Quantities, and the Chemical Potential
item.description: Lecture notes about criteria for equilibrium, multicomponent systems, partial molar quantities, and the chemical potential.

item.title: Partition Functions
item.description: Lecture notes on partition functions, examples of macroscopic thermodynamic results, ideal gas mixture, and ideal liquid mixture.

item.title: Partition Functions
item.description: Lecture notes on partition functions, examples of macroscopic thermodynamic results, ideal gas mixture, and ideal liquid mixture.

item.title: Phase Equilibria in a 1-Component System
item.description: Lecture notes about phase equilibria in a 1-component system.

item.title: Phase Equilibria in a 1-Component System
item.description: Lecture notes about phase equilibria in a 1-component system.

item.title: Reversible Adiabatic Expansion (or compression) of an Ideal Gas
item.description: Lecture notes on reversible adiabatic expansion (or compression) of an ideal gas, irreversible adiabatic expansion of an ideal gas against a constant external pressure, and thermodynamic cycles.

item.title: Reversible Adiabatic Expansion (or compression) of an Ideal Gas
item.description: Lecture notes on reversible adiabatic expansion (or compression) of an ideal gas, irreversible adiabatic expansion of an ideal gas against a constant external pressure, and thermodynamic cycles.

item.title: Statistical Mechanics
item.description: Lecture notes with an introduction to statistical mechanics and discussion of probability of system in a state with given energy, partition functions, thermodynamic properties from partition functions, and entropy in terms of probabilities and degeneracies.

item.title: Statistical Mechanics
item.description: Lecture notes with an introduction to statistical mechanics and discussion of probability of system in a state with given energy, partition functions, thermodynamic properties from partition functions, and entropy in terms of probabilities and degeneracies.

item.title: Statistical Thermodynamics
item.description: Lecture notes on statistical thermodynamics, calculation of macroscopic thermodynamic results, ideal gas mixtures, ideal liquid mixtures, energy, and entropy changes.

item.title: Temperature Dependence of Rate Constant
item.description: Lecture notes on temperature dependence, the Arrhenius law, and catalysis.

item.title: Temperature Dependence of Rate Constant
item.description: Lecture notes on temperature dependence, the Arrhenius law, and catalysis.

item.title: The Gibbs Free Energy
item.description: Lecture notes on the Gibbs free energy and third law absolute entropy.

item.title: The Gibbs Free Energy
item.description: Lecture notes on the Gibbs free energy and third law absolute entropy.

item.title: The Second Law
item.description: Lecture notes on the second law of thermodynamics.

item.title: The Second Law
item.description: Lecture notes on the second law of thermodynamics.

item.title: Thermochemistry
item.description: Lecture notes on thermochemistry.

item.title: Thermochemistry
item.description: Lecture notes on thermochemistry.

item.title: Thermodynamics
item.description: Lecture notes on thermodynamics, the four laws, and equations of state.

item.title: Thermodynamics
item.description: Lecture notes on thermodynamics, the four laws, and equations of state.

item.title: Two-Component Phase Equilibria
item.description: Lecture notes on two-component phase equilibria, binary liquid-gas mixtures, Raoult’s law, and ideal solutions.

item.title: Two-Component Phase Equilibria
item.description: Lecture notes on two-component phase equilibria, binary liquid-gas mixtures, Raoult’s law, and ideal solutions.

item.title: Two-Component Phase Equilibria II: Ideal and Non-Ideal Solutions
item.description: Lecture notes on two-component phase equilibria, ideal solutions, and non-ideal solutions.

item.title: Two-Component Phase Equilibria II: Ideal and Non-Ideal Solutions
item.description: Lecture notes on two-component phase equilibria, ideal solutions, and non-ideal solutions.

item.title: Two-Component Phase Equilibria II: Ideal and Non-Ideal Solutions
item.description: Lecture notes on two-component phase equilibria, ideal solutions, and non-ideal solutions.

item.title: Work, Heat, and the First Law
item.description: Lecture notes on work, heat, and the first law of thermodynamics.

item.title: Work, Heat, and the First Law
item.description: Lecture notes on work, heat, and the first law of thermodynamics.
